user_question: In the global atomics, are the slots for dKV pre-buffered as fixed (g,h,d) destinations, one hxd matrix per compressed group g?

answer.layout_correction: For the current shared latent-KV sparse MLA backward, the fixed destination is effectively dKV[seq_len + group, d], not dKV[group, head, d]. The head dimension is a reduction dimension.
answer.reason: Each selected compressed group g maps to one shared latent KV row. Contributions from all query tokens, selected slots, and heads accumulate into that row and D element.

math_contract.formula: dKV[g,d] += sum_{token,k,h: safe_groups[token,k] == g} probs[token,h,k] * grad_output[token,h,d] + scaled_dP[token,h,k] * q[token,h,d]
math_contract.forward_sidecar: safe_groups/group_ids[B,S,K] is already the forward-known compressed group sidecar consumed by the current Triton dot_k_atomic path.
math_contract.output_allocation: The global gradient buffer is preallocated before accumulation; global atomics add into known rows rather than allocating or discovering output slots.

atomic_shape.read: One indirect global atomic is issued per selected contribution and D element after the K64 dot contribution is produced.

failed_shortcuts.reason: Group-owned matching repeats/replays contribution production and loses the fast query-major tl.dot schedule.
Keeps the fast producer but inserts a global partial-row load/store per valid selected contribution before final reduction.
Carries exact group ids but still uses token_block=1, so it provides no local write-count reduction and adds producer/replay cost.
Moves too much of the fast Triton producer into a slower 16-wave CTA owner; final atomics are not the measured wall in that implementation.

stage_mapping.scatter_add: VecOp::ScatterAdd reads mm4/mm5 workspace rows, scales dk, adds dv, then uses SetAtomicAdd<float>() and DataCopy into d_ori_kv/d_cmp_kv rows.

ascend_reference_read.amd_lesson: Ascend does not avoid final atomic scatter. Its win is that Cube345 creates row-like partials and ScatterAdd consumes staged rows with vector ping-pong, rather than issuing a raw per-contribution indirect atomic stream from the producer.

next_kernel_contract.goal: Keep the Triton-like query-major K64 dot producer, but add on-chip segmented local reduction by exact group id before global atomics.
required_shape: wider token tile than 1 so the epilogue has duplicate group destinations to reduce
q/dO/probs/scaled_dP must remain resident or be consumed immediately
exact safe_groups sidecar is carried into the epilogue
emit one reduced atomic per locally accumulated (group,d) row instead of per (token,selected_slot,d) contribution where possible
rejected_as_not_enough: adding group ids as metadata without widening the local reduction tile
detaching ScatterAdd into a separate raw HBM partial slab
making compressed group the outer contribution loop when it reloads q/dO too often
next_kernel_contract.promotion_gate: A S4096 source-boundary sparse attention backward candidate must beat the 11.13 ms full Triton gate, then materialize into 4x/8x TorchTitan TFLOPs and memory before promotion.
